name: North Carolina
agency: North Carolina Secretary of State, Corporations Division

llc.formName: Articles of Dissolution of Limited Liability Company
steps: Vote to dissolve according to your operating agreement or by majority vote of members
Wind up business affairs and settle all debts and obligations
Notify all known creditors in writing
File final state tax returns with the NC Department of Revenue
Submit NC-BN Out-of-Business Notification to the Department of Revenue
File Articles of Dissolution (Form L-07) with the Secretary of State ($30 fee)
Cancel any licenses, permits, and assumed business names
llc.notes: North Carolina does not require tax clearance before filing dissolution. Form L-07 can be filed online or by mail.

steps: Board of directors adopts a resolution recommending dissolution
Shareholders approve dissolution by majority vote
Notify all known creditors and claimants in writing
Wind up business affairs, settle debts, and distribute remaining assets
File final state tax returns with the NC Department of Revenue
Submit NC-BN Out-of-Business Notification to the Department of Revenue
File Articles of Dissolution (Form B-06) with the Secretary of State ($30 fee)
Cancel all licenses, permits, and assumed business names
corporation.notes: Use Form B-05 ($30) if dissolving before shares have been issued. Use Form B-06 ($30) for dissolution after shares have been issued, which requires director and shareholder approval.

tax.notes: North Carolina does not require tax clearance before dissolution. However, you must file all final tax returns for income, sales, and withholding taxes. Submit the NC-BN Out-of-Business Notification form to the Department of Revenue to close your tax accounts.

requirements.employeeNotification: Must comply with federal WARN Act for layoffs of 100+ employees. Provide final paychecks by next regular payday.
requirements.creditorNotification: Must notify all known creditors and claimants in writing with a deadline of at least 120 days to submit claims
requirements.registeredAgentNotes: Registered agent must be maintained until dissolution is complete and all pending matters are resolved
requirements.windingUpPeriod: Entity continues to exist for purposes of winding up after dissolution is filed
requirements.boardOrMemberApproval: LLCs require member vote per operating agreement. Corporations require board resolution and shareholder majority approval.

includes: Every required form with direct download links
